title: Upstream: Selected Essays
description: Mary Oliver's collected essays on poetry, attention, and the natural world.

title: Wild Geese
description: A landmark poem and broader collection on forgiveness, belonging, and the natural world.

title: Devotions: The Selected Poems of Mary Oliver
description: A comprehensive retrospective of Oliver's most beloved poems over five decades.

title: Blessing the Boats: New and Selected Poems 1988-2000
description: National Book Award-winning collection drawing on Clifton's life, history, and Black womanhood.

title: How to Carry Water: Selected Poems of Lucille Clifton
description: A generous retrospective of Clifton's poetry edited by Aracelis Girmay.

title: Kindred
description: A modern Black woman is repeatedly drawn back in time to an antebellum Maryland plantation.

title: Parable of the Sower
description: A young Black woman builds a new religion while surviving a collapsing near-future California.

title: Parable of the Talents
description: The continuation of Lauren Olamina's struggle to found Earthseed amid theocratic oppression.

title: Behind the Beautiful Forevers
description: National Book Award winner about life and aspiration in an Mumbai slum.

title: The Emperor of All Maladies: A Biography of Cancer
description: Pulitzer Prize-winning history of cancer and its treatment across the centuries.

title: The Gene: An Intimate History
description: A sweeping narrative of the science of heredity from Mendel to CRISPR.

title: The Laws of Medicine
description: Three essential laws governing medical uncertainty and decision-making.

title: Mountains Beyond Mountains
description: Profile of Paul Farmer and his quest to bring quality health care to the world's poorest.

title: Epidemiology: An Introduction
description: Ken Rothman's accessible graduate-level introduction to epidemiological methods.

title: Modern Epidemiology
description: The standard graduate reference on epidemiologic theory and methods, now in its fourth edition.

title: Just Mercy
description: Bryan Stevenson's memoir of defending death-row prisoners and confronting systemic injustice.

title: An American Marriage
description: Tayari Jones's Oprah Book Club novel about a Black couple whose marriage is tested by wrongful imprisonment.

title: Homegoing
description: Yaa Gyasi's debut novel traces two Ghanaian half-sisters' lineages across three centuries.

title: The Warmth of Other Suns
description: Isabel Wilkerson's epic account of the Great Migration of Black Americans from the South.

title: Braiding Sweetgrass
description: Robin Wall Kimmerer weaves Indigenous plant knowledge with Western science and poetic vision.

title: The Immortal Life of Henrietta Lacks
description: Rebecca Skloot's account of the Black woman whose cancer cells became a cornerstone of modern medicine.

title: Caste: The Origins of Our Discontents
description: Isabel Wilkerson examines the unspoken caste system structuring American social hierarchy.
